environment_api.reset_signature: env.reset() -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]  (single-game) or -> Tuple[List[str], Dict[str, List[Any]]]  (batch)
environment_api.step_signature: env.step(command: str) -> Tuple[str, float, bool, Dict[str, Any]]  (single-game: obs, score, done, infos) or env.step(commands: List[str]) -> Tuple[List[str], List[float], List[bool], Dict[str, List[Any]]]  (batch)

fields.name: feedback
fields.source: textworld/envs/pddl/pddl.py:127 (reset), textworld/envs/pddl/pddl.py:158-162 (step); returned as first element of (obs, score, done, infos) tuple via textworld/envs/wrappers/filter.py:47-48

action_schema.parser: textworld/envs/pddl/pddl.py:145 — command.strip() is looked up via list.index() in self.prev_state['_valid_commands']; ValueError raised if not found, which triggers 'Nothing happens.' feedback

info_fields.name: won
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset; True when PDDL goal is satisfied

info_fields.name: admissible_commands
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset; derived from all currently applicable PDDL actions

info_fields.name: facts
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset when request_infos.facts=True; full set of PDDL world propositions

info_fields.name: policy_commands
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset when request_infos.policy_commands=True; planner re-plans from current state each call

info_fields.name: extra.gamefile
info_fields.populated_when: reset; path to the current game.tw-pddl file via AlfredInfos wrapper

info_fields.name: extra.expert_plan
info_fields.populated_when: reset and step when AlfredExpert wrapper is applied; contains one next action from the handcoded expert

info_fields.name: extra.walkthrough
info_fields.populated_when: reset when extras=['walkthrough'] requested; full solution sequence from game file or re-planned by planner

info_fields.name: description
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset when request_infos.description=True; text output of look command

info_fields.name: inventory
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset when request_infos.inventory=True; text listing of held objects

info_fields.name: location
info_fields.populated_when: every step and reset when request_infos.location=True; name of player's current location

info_fields.name: moves
info_fields.populated_when: every step when request_infos.moves=True; count of valid commands executed

info_fields.name: goal_condition_success_rate
info_fields.populated_when: every step in ThorEnv path only (alfred_thor_env.py:178); ratio of goal conditions met

reward_signature.range: [0, 1] sparse binary; 1.0 only on the step when won becomes True

done_signature.source: textworld/envs/pddl/pddl.py:171 — self.state['done'] = self.state['won'] or self.state['lost']; augmented by textworld/envs/wrappers/limit.py (Limit wrapper) which sets done=True after max_episode_steps
done_signature.semantics: True when: (a) PDDL goal is satisfied (won=True), or (b) game is lost (lost always False in PDDL backend), or (c) max_episode_steps exceeded by the Limit wrapper

official_metrics.name: success_rate
official_metrics.entry: alfworld/agents/eval/evaluate_dagger.py:evaluate_dagger — uses infos['won'] to compute game_points per episode

official_metrics.name: average_game_points
official_metrics.entry: alfworld/agents/eval/evaluate_dagger.py:evaluate_dagger — max(sequence_game_points) per episode, where game_points = max 1.0 (won) or 0.0

official_metrics.name: average_goal_condition_points
official_metrics.entry: alfworld/agents/eval/evaluate_dagger.py:evaluate_dagger — uses infos['goal_condition_success_rate'] (only available in ThorEnv path)

oracle_candidates.name: won_info_field
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Exact binary signal: True iff the PDDL goal check passes, matching the benchmark's success criterion precisely.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Using won as a dense reward signal instead of episode-level binary label will produce a delayed sparse signal that only fires on the final winning step.

oracle_candidates.name: score_info_field
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Numerically identical to won (1.0 or 0.0); same semantics but returned as float rather than bool.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Identical to won_info_field; treating this as a continuous signal is misleading since it is binary.

oracle_candidates.name: pddl_facts_state
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Exposes the complete symbolic world state as a list of Propositions; a downstream oracle can evaluate any predicate over this set to detect intermediate goal progress.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: PDDL entity names in facts use internal IDs unless AlfredDemangler is applied; comparing facts against human-readable object names without demangling will fail silently.

oracle_candidates.name: policy_commands_plan
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Provides the PDDL-planner's optimal action sequence from the current state; re-planned at every step so always reflects remaining optimal path.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Leaks the solution path directly; providing this field in the agent observation defeats the benchmark's challenge.

oracle_candidates.name: expert_plan_handcoded
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Handcoded heuristic expert provides a single next-step action recommendation per state; less brittle than planner but may time out (HandCodedAgentTimeout) on complex states.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Expert may return 'look' as a fallback when it cannot determine the next action; treating 'look' as always a valid oracle signal is incorrect.

oracle_candidates.name: traj_data_task_annotation
oracle_candidates.source_location: assets at ~/.cache/alfworld/json_2.1.1/**/traj_data.json; keys: pddl_params, plan, task_type
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Per-game structured annotation: task type, object/parent/toggle targets (pddl_params), high-level PDDL plan, and low-level action sequence; provides ground-truth for what the task is asking.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Must be correlated via extra.gamefile info field; path joining logic is fragile if data is relocated or split names differ.

oracle_candidates.name: admissible_commands_validity_heuristic
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Enumerates all currently executable PDDL actions; can serve as a binary oracle for action validity (action in admissible_commands implies no 'Nothing happens.' feedback).
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: admissible_commands reflects PDDL-executable actions but does not distinguish task-relevant from task-irrelevant ones; membership is necessary but not sufficient for goal progress.

oracle_candidates.name: goal_condition_success_rate_thor
oracle_candidates.fidelity_to_goal: Fractional measure of sub-goals satisfied (pcs[0] / float(pcs[1])) per task class; more granular than binary won signal.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Only available when using AlfredThorEnv (visual simulator); not exposed in the text-only AlfredTWEnv path that is the primary ALFWorld interface.

feedback_generation_points.condition: command not in prev_state['_valid_commands'] (ValueError from list.index())
CleanObject, HeatObject, CoolObject, or ToggleObject action succeeds

latent_state_channels.name: pddl_facts
latent_state_channels.access: Set request_infos.facts=True when calling textworld.gym.register_games(); available as info['facts'] (List[Proposition]) after each step/reset

latent_state_channels.name: policy_commands
latent_state_channels.access: Set request_infos.policy_commands=True; available as info['policy_commands'] (List[str]); PDDL planner re-plans at each step from current state

latent_state_channels.name: walkthrough
latent_state_channels.access: Pass extras=['walkthrough'] in EnvInfos; available as info['extra.walkthrough'] (List[str]); value comes from game file's stored walkthrough or PDDL re-plan

latent_state_channels.name: expert_plan
latent_state_channels.access: Apply AlfredExpert wrapper (alfred_tw_env.py:60-110) with AlfredExpertType.HANDCODED or PLANNER; available as info['extra.expert_plan'] (List[str] with one action)

latent_state_channels.name: traj_data_pddl_params
latent_state_channels.access: Read traj_data.json from path derived via info['extra.gamefile']; keys: pddl_params (object/parent/toggle targets), task_type, plan (high_pddl, low_actions)

minimal_runnable_commands: python3 -c "import textworld, textworld.gym; from alfworld.agents.environment.alfred_tw_env import AlfredDemangler, AlfredInfos; import glob; gf = glob.glob('/home/yuhan/.cache/alfworld/json_2.1.1/valid_train/**/*.tw-pddl', recursive=True)[:1]; ri = textworld.EnvInfos(won=True, admissible_commands=True, facts=True, extras=['gamefile']); eid = textworld.gym.register_games(gf, ri, batch_size=1, asynchronous=False, max_episode_steps=50, wrappers=[AlfredDemangler, AlfredInfos]); env = textworld.gym.make(eid); obs, infos = env.reset(); print('obs:', obs[:100]); obs2, score, done, infos2 = env.step(infos['admissible_commands'][0]); print('step obs:', obs2[:80], 'score:', score, 'done:', done); env.close()"
open_questions: Does the PDDL backend ever set state['lost'] = True in practice, or is it always False as the comment in pddl.py:77 suggests?
Is goal_condition_success_rate available in the AlfredTWEnv (text-only) path, or only in the AlfredThorEnv (visual) path?
What is the exact set of grammar feedback rules for CleanObject, HeatObject, CoolObject, SliceObject, and ToggleObject actions (these were not fully inspected from the grammar JSON)?
Are there any game files where the 'walkthrough' key in game.tw-pddl is populated vs. requiring re-planning?
Does the Limit wrapper (max_episode_steps) trigger done=True silently without any feedback text change, i.e., does the agent receive a final observation different from a normal step?
